tagline: Searches prior art, screens bars and drafts the filing — a USPTO-registered patent practitioner signs before it is filed with the USPTO.
problem: Patent prosecution is high-stakes novelty/obviousness analysis under a duty of candor, where a missed statutory bar or a candor breach forfeits or invalidates the rights and only a registered practitioner may prosecute before the USPTO.
outcome: Disclosures searched, screened and drafted straight-through; a USPTO-registered patent practitioner signs before the application/response is filed with the USPTO.

steps.does: Intake the invention disclosure (spec, drawings, inventor declarations)
Search prior art and assess patentability (102 novelty / 103 obviousness / 112) + inventorship
Screen statutory bars + duty-of-candor / IDS + foreign-filing-license / export
A USPTO-registered patent practitioner signs the application / response
File the application / response with the USPTO
Docket and monitor the application (status, deadlines, office actions)
